title: Question: Verilator Speed
body:
---


Author Name: **Felix Bors** (@felixbors)
Original Redmine Message: 1896 from https://www.veripool.org



---

I have the following very simple module counting up and calling $finish.
In Modelsim  6.6 (Windows) this takes 6 seconds (with the test bench attached).
Under Linux/Verilator this takes about 21 seconds.  I'm wondering if I do something
wrong or if I miss something - in particular I evaluate the simulator twice
per input clock (on each edge) maybe that's not correct...
Attached are the our.v (verilog module) our_tb.v (the Verilog testbench) and
sim_main.cpp (the Verilator testbench).
I build the code like this:

verilator -Wall -O3 --cc our.v --exe sim_main.cpp
cd obj_dir
make -j -f Vour.mk Vour

Thank you
